name: Cattails Golf Club
about: Cattails Golf Club is an 18-hole championship public golf course designed by PGA Professional John R. Williams and opened in 1991. The facility integrates natural wetlands and hardwood forests into its layout, with the front nine navigating creeks and wetlands, and the back nine cut through a hardwood forest. The venue also offers extensive indoor golf simulator options and a full-service grill. As a daily-fee course, it provides superior personal service, outstanding course conditions, and a variety of amenities for players in the Detroit area.
why_golfers_like_it: Extensive indoor golf simulator options for year-round play
Championship 18-hole course designed by a PGA professional
Hosts major events like the LPGA Futures Tour and Michigan Amateur Qualifier
Offers both house leagues and new league opportunities for social play
Natural, scenic course design that integrates wetlands and forests

simulator_hardware.notes: Multiple AboutGolf simulators available based on content

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: The website doesn't specify, but it's standard practice to bring your own clubs. Some rental options may be available.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: The Cattails Grill is available on-site, but the website doesn't specify if outside food and drinks are permitted.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: With proper pace, the course should take no more than 4 hours and 10 minutes to play as rated by the U.S.G.A.

faq.question: Is the facility kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, Cattails Golf Club appears to be family-friendly, offering leagues and events that would appeal to junior golfers.
